articles.description: An unusually hot, snowless winter has fueled a fast start to the spring fire season across large swaths of the western, central and southeastern US, disrupting businesses and upending daily life.

articles.title: Tambaram, Chennai’s gateway, is booming, but civic infrastructure issues remain
articles.description: Tambaram, often called Chennai’s gateway, is witnessing rapid growth and urban expansion. But residents say civic infrastructure — from roads and drainage to public services — is struggling to keep pace. Voters are demanding better planning and stronger infrastructure to match Tambaram’s rise.

articles.title: Kerala filmmaker Ranjith gets bail in sexual harassment case
articles.description: As part of bail conditions, court directs Ranjith to surrender his passport, remain within Ernakulam district, and refrain from entering Fort Kochi, where the alleged offence took place during a film shoot,

articles.title: Woman ends life weeks after partner’s arrest for injecting her HIV-positive blood
Gurugram founder fires employee on WhatsApp, 'no-notice' policy sparks backlash
articles.description: Gurugram founder fires employee on WhatsApp nonotice policy sparks backlash

articles.title: World Parkinson’s Day awareness programme in Guntur
Kerala polls: UDF will return to power with 100 plus seats: Satheesan
articles.description: He said an increasing number of pre-poll surveys, including those held at the national level, had predicted an electoral outcome favouring the UDF.

topStories.title: Wildfires Race Across US as Drought Spans Half the Nation
topStories.description: An unusually hot, snowless winter has fueled a fast start to the spring fire season across large swaths of the western, central and southeastern US, disrupting businesses and upending daily life.

topStories.title: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, dies week after he was injured in US-Israeli strikes
topStories.description: Kamal Kharazi, former Iranian foreign minister, died from injuries sustained in US-Israeli airstrikes on April 1. He was 81 and head of the Strategic Council for International Relations. He previously represented Iran at the UN in New York and later served as foreign minister from 1997 to 2005

topStories.title: Pakistan preps for make-or-break Washington-Tehran meeting - but will Iran show up? All you need to know
topStories.description: Pakistan is preparing to host talks between US and Iranian delegations on Saturday, though Iran's participation is uncertain due to recent Israeli strikes in Lebanon. The agenda includes Iran's nuclear program and the Strait of Hormuz, with discussions likely to be indirect.

topStories.title: Senegal PM labels Trump ‘agent of global destabilisation’
topStories.description: Senegalese prime minister Ousmane Sonko accused Donald Trump of plunging the world into “chaos”.

topStories.title: Six months into ‘ceasefire’, Gaza suffers under persistent Israeli attacks
topStories.description: Gaza's devastation grows as over 72,000 people killed and 172,000 injured amid Israeli violations of US-brokered truce.

topStories.title: "Grave Concern": Supreme Court On Indians Forced Into Russia-Ukraine War
topStories.description: An advocate told NDTV that there are hundreds of such cases where Indians are lured by agents on the pretext of lucrative job promises but are pushed into the war zone.

topStories.title: US waiver on Russian oil sanctions expires on 11 April — Will US extend the relaxations amid talks with Iran?
topStories.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin's special envoy Kirill Dmitriev is currently in the US, meeting US President Donald Trump's administration for discussions on a peace deal with Ukraine. It was not clear whether the talks will extend to the sanctions.

topStories.title: Iran ceasefire: Not an off-ramp for the US but a life-saving ejection seat
topStories.description: A Middle Eastern country has for the first time single-handedly checked the war-making capabilities of the US and Israel

topStories.title: Bessent, Powell warn bank CEOs about Anthropic's new ‘Mythos’ AI model — What risks did they flag?
topStories.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell met with bank CEOs to discuss cybersecurity risks from Anthropic's Mythos AI model.

topStories.title: Ex-Congress leader alleges abduction bid by police after being stopped for ‘traffic violation’ in Shimla
topStories.description: The incident took place near the Congress headquarters here, when the police personnel stopped the vehicle of Yudhveer Singh Bains for checking.

topStories.title: Japanese tankers head closer to Strait of Hormuz as more ships gather after US-Iran ceasefire
topStories.description: With hundreds of vessels stuck since US and Israeli strikes began at the end of February, some are now beginning to shift positions to be nearer to Hormuz. A ceasefire has pushed shipowners to begin considering options, though most say conditions are still too unclear to attempt an exit.

topStories.title: Iran-US war: Tehran denies reports of negotiators travelling to Pakistan for talks
topStories.description: Pakistan's capital is on high alert with 10,000 security personnel deployed. JD Vance is set to lead talks in Islamabad amid a fragile ceasefire between Iran and the US.

dailyNews.description: Republican Rep. Randy Fine, who represents Florida's 6th Congressional District, has announced that he joined the conservative House Freedom Caucus.

dailyNews.title: American couple's Bahamas dinghy was ill-equipped for conditions night of wife's disappearance: friend
dailyNews.description: A friend says Brian and Lynette Hooker's 8-foot dinghy was undersized and underpowered for the rough conditions the night she vanished in the Bahamas.

dailyNews.title: Simple fruit combo could improve heart function in weeks, new study finds
dailyNews.description: A new study found that adults with prediabetes who ate one avocado and one cup of mango daily for eight weeks saw improvements in vascular function, though experts note caveats.

dailyNews.title: Fox News Digital's News Quiz: April 10, 2026
dailyNews.description: This week's News Quiz covers Gov. Abigail Spanberger dodging DHS questions and Twitch streamer Hasan Piker accusing a media figure of anti-Blackness and Islamophobia.

dailyNews.title: Our fallen heroes' families deserve more than outdated survivor benefits
dailyNews.description: Gold Star families face emotional and financial hardship after losing a loved one in service. New legislation aims to increase the death gratuity.

dailyNews.description: As the Trump administration winds down the Department of Education, a school choice revolution gains momentum with over 1.3 million students enrolled.

dailyNews.title: DoorDash data offers snapshot of economy voters are feeling ahead of midterms
dailyNews.description: A new DoorDash report reveals families are spending more strategically as prices stabilize, with affordability varying significantly across U.S. cities.

dailyNews.title: Hormuz choke point persists as Iran halts oil traffic despite Trump ceasefire
dailyNews.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed despite a ceasefire deal, with 800 tankers among 3,200 vessels backed up and mariners stranded.

dailyNews.description: A Digital Flight Authorization System could replace fragmented drone approval processes with real-time verification of operators and missions.

dailyNews.description: Artemis II is set to splash down off San Diego as Navy crews prepare to recover the Orion capsule and the four astronauts inside, drawing crowds along the coast.

dailyNews.title: 4/9: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: President Trump says he asked Netanyahu to scale back Lebanon strikes; Melania Trump makes a rare statement denying relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

dailyNews.title: Trump criticizes NATO after talks with Rutte
dailyNews.description: President Trump bashed NATO after meeting with Secretary General Mark Rutte on Wednesday. Ken Thomas, national politics reporter for the Wall Street Journal and Dave Levinthal, senior editor for NOTUS, joined "The Takeout" to discuss that and some of the day's other top political headlines.

dailyNews.title: White House staff received email warning not to place bets on prediction markets
dailyNews.description: White House aides got an email last month telling them not to place bets on prediction markets with nonpublic information, multiple administration officials told CBS News.

dailyNews.title: A mother mourning the son she lost in the Iran war says she told Trump: 'Go get 'em'
dailyNews.description: More than a month after her son, Maj. Cody Khork, died in the Iran war, Donna Burhans has found a routine that helps her cope.

dailyNews.title: Exclusive: Cuban president reacts to Trump admin’s call for him to step down
dailyNews.description: In an exclusive interview with Meet the Press, Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel says "stepping down [is] not part of our vocabulary" amid reports that the U.S. government has asked the Cuban leader to resign.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: U.S. oil climbs above $100 as U.S.-Iran ceasefire fails to boost tanker traffic via Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: U.S. President Donald Trump on Thursday warned Iran to "stop now" if it was charging tankers to transit the Strait of Hormuz.
